summary: LaunchSpec parameter pre-caching successfully reduces Python dispatch overhead from ~21μs to ~0.12μs per launch (184x speedup per launch), yielding 2.24x end-to-end decode throughput improvement (9.78 → 21.92 tok/s). Correctness validated with cosine sim 0.999972-0.999995 vs threshold 0.999.

codeQualityIssues.description: The threaded dispatch infrastructure (_worker_threads, _go_events, _done_events, etc.) was implemented and kept in TPInferenceEngine but is documented as counter-productive (490μs/round overhead × 128 rounds = 63ms penalty). This dead code adds ~100 lines of complexity.
codeQualityIssues.suggestedFix: Consider removing or disabling the threaded dispatch path since it is known slower. At minimum, the docstring should clearly say 'DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION — use cached dispatch instead'.

codeQualityIssues.description: LaunchSpec.params_array uses C void* pointers to ctypes values that must remain alive. If a caller accidentally modifies the params list reference, the params_array becomes invalid (dangling pointer). No guard against this.
codeQualityIssues.suggestedFix: This is by design and documented in the docstring, but could add a tuple conversion for the internal params storage to prevent accidental mutation: self.params = list(params) (already done).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md library file already documents the stream overlap (Milestone 2) findings and P2P race condition fix in detail. The cached dispatch results (23.7 tok/s) are documented. The library is up to date for this feature.

testCoverage: test_threaded_dispatch.py: 10 correctness steps (cosine sim all >= 0.999), 10 performance bench steps showing 2.240x speedup. test_dispatch_overhead.py: ctypes overhead microbenchmark showing 184x reduction.
